keyEvents.impact: Continental nation-state acutely aware of its small population and vast, exposed geography
ANZAC founding myth; tradition of fighting alongside great-power allies far from home

keyEvents.event: Fall of Singapore and bombing of Darwin
keyEvents.impact: Britain could not protect Australia; decisive strategic pivot to the United States
US alliance becomes the bedrock of security policy for every government since

keyEvents.event: Leads INTERFET intervention in East Timor
keyEvents.impact: Accepted responsibility as security provider in its immediate neighborhood
Punitive tariffs after calling for COVID inquiry; resolve to resist economic blackmail
Nuclear-powered submarine program; deepest-ever technological integration with the US and UK

history.narrative: An island continent whose prosperity flows across sea lanes dominated by its biggest customer and biggest worry: China. It hedges through the ANZUS alliance, AUKUS submarines, and Quad diplomacy, acting as the principal security partner of the South Pacific while trying to keep trade with Beijing alive without yielding to coercion.

redLines.description: Hostile interdiction of the sea lanes carrying Australian trade
Establishment of a hostile military base in the South Pacific islands
Armed attack on the United States or key Indo-Pacific partners

internalDivisions.name: Climate policy vs mining economy divide

goals.description: Deliver AUKUS nuclear-powered submarines and deepen US alliance integration
Diversify trade to reduce vulnerability to Chinese economic coercion
Remain the security partner of choice in the South Pacific and block rival basing
Protect the maritime trade routes on which the island economy depends
